title: adr-kit ADR Enforcement block
description: Schema for the optional ## Enforcement section in an ADR (introduced in adr-kit v0.12.0). The block is a fenced JSON code block parsed by bin/adr-judge to apply declarative rules to a staged git diff at commit time. ADRs without an Enforcement block are skipped silently by the judge. Free-form ADRs that cannot be expressed as regex can opt into in-session LLM review by setting llm_judge:true.

forbid_pattern.description: Regex patterns that must NOT match any ADDED line in the diff (lines starting with '+' that are not '+++ '). Treated as a violation when matched.

require_pattern.description: Regex patterns that MUST match at least once in the post-diff content of any file matching path_glob. Treated as a violation when absent.

forbid_import.description: Forbidden import / include / require lines. Semantically the same engine as forbid_pattern; the separate name documents intent ('we forbid this dependency') and lets reviewers spot dependency-removal rules at a glance.

llm_judge.description: If true, this ADR is reviewed by Claude in the active Claude Code session via /adr-kit:judge. The pre-commit hook treats llm_judge:true as advisory: it surfaces a one-liner reminding the user to run /adr-kit:judge but does NOT fail the commit. Use for free-form ADRs whose rules cannot be expressed as regex.

pattern.description: Python re.search regex (escape backslashes correctly when embedding in JSON: '\\b', '\\.'). Applied per added line (forbid_pattern, forbid_import) or per post-diff file content (require_pattern). Multiline is not enabled by default.

path_glob.description: Optional glob filter (supports ** for recursive descent). When set, the rule only applies to files in the diff whose path matches the glob. Default: all files in the diff.

message.description: Optional human-friendly message printed when the rule fires. Defaults to a generic '<rule_kind>: <pattern>' line.
